title: Parallel Branch and Merge
constraints: Sentiment analysis and entity extraction must run in parallel, not sequentially
Both branches must complete before the merge step begins
The merge step must combine results from both branches into a single structured output
The parallel execution must produce a measurable speedup over sequential processing
hints: In LangGraph, use a fan-out pattern: connect the input node to both analysis nodes, then connect both to a merge node
Store each branch's result in a separate key of the shared state so the merge node can access both
In CrewAI, use async tasks or parallel task execution to run both analyses concurrently

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage
import time

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Sentiment and entity extraction run sequentially, doubling latency
# TODO: Run both analyses in parallel and merge results
def analyze_text(text: str) -> dict:
    start = time.time()

    sentiment = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Analyze the sentiment of this text. Reply with: positive, negative, or neutral, followed by a brief explanation."),
        HumanMessage(content=text),
    ])

    entities = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Extract all named entities (people, organizations, locations, products) from this text. Return as a JSON list."),
        HumanMessage(content=text),
    ])

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return {
        "sentiment": sentiment.content,
        "entities": entities.content,
        "processing_time": f"{elapsed:.2f}s",
    }

texts = [
    "Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the new iPhone at their Cupertino headquarters. Customers loved it.",
    "The project in Berlin failed miserably. Google and Microsoft both pulled out of the partnership.",
]
for text in texts:
    result = analyze_text(text)
    print(f"Text: {text[:50]}...\nResult: {result}\n")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
import time

# BUG: Sentiment and entity extraction run sequentially, doubling latency
# TODO: Run both analyses in parallel and merge results
analyst = Agent(
    role="Text Analyst",
    goal="Analyze text for both sentiment and entities",
    backstory="You are an NLP specialist.",
)

def analyze_text(text: str) -> dict:
    start = time.time()

    sentiment_task = Task(
        description=f"Analyze the sentiment of this text: '{text}'. Reply with positive, negative, or neutral and a brief explanation.",
        expected_output="Sentiment label and explanation",
        agent=analyst,
    )

    entity_task = Task(
        description=f"Extract all named entities (people, organizations, locations, products) from: '{text}'. Return as a JSON list.",
        expected_output="JSON list of entities",
        agent=analyst,
    )

    crew = Crew(agents=[analyst], tasks=[sentiment_task, entity_task])
    results = crew.kickoff()

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return {"results": str(results), "processing_time": f"{elapsed:.2f}s"}

texts = [
    "Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the new iPhone at their Cupertino headquarters. Customers loved it.",
    "The project in Berlin failed miserably. Google and Microsoft both pulled out of the partnership.",
]
for text in texts:
    result = analyze_text(text)
    print(f"Text: {text[:50]}...\nResult: {result}\n")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
import time

# BUG: Sentiment and entity extraction run sequentially, doubling latency
# TODO: Run both analyses in parallel and merge results
def analyze_text(text: str) -> dict:
    start = time.time()

    sentiment_agent = Agent(
        name="Sentiment Analyst",
        instructions="Analyze the sentiment of the given text. Reply with: positive, negative, or neutral, followed by a brief explanation.",
    )
    sentiment_result = Runner.run_sync(sentiment_agent, text)

    entity_agent = Agent(
        name="Entity Extractor",
        instructions="Extract all named entities (people, organizations, locations, products) from the given text. Return as a JSON list.",
    )
    entity_result = Runner.run_sync(entity_agent, text)

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return {
        "sentiment": sentiment_result.final_output,
        "entities": entity_result.final_output,
        "processing_time": f"{elapsed:.2f}s",
    }

texts = [
    "Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the new iPhone at their Cupertino headquarters. Customers loved it.",
    "The project in Berlin failed miserably. Google and Microsoft both pulled out of the partnership.",
]
for text in texts:
    result = analyze_text(text)
    print(f"Text: {text[:50]}...\nResult: {result}\n")

evaluationCriteria.label: Parallel execution
evaluationCriteria.description: Sentiment analysis and entity extraction run concurrently, not one after the other

evaluationCriteria.label: Both complete before merge
evaluationCriteria.description: The merge step waits for both branches to finish before combining results

evaluationCriteria.label: Structured merged output
evaluationCriteria.description: The final output contains both sentiment and entities in a single structured result
